factors.summary: Free cash flow generation is improving due to higher operating cash flow and lower investing activities.

evidence.text: We generated $104.2 million and $100.4 million of cash flow from operations during the three months ended March 31, 2016 and March 31, 2015, an increase of $3.8 million compared to the three months ended March 31, 2015.
evidence.subsection: LIQUIDITY AND CAPITAL RESOURCES

factors.summary: The company is maintaining its shareholder returns with a focus on dividends and share repurchases, which are consistent with management's confidence.

evidence.text: We used $63.9 million and $57.3 million to pay cash dividends during the three months ended March 31, 2016 and March 31, 2015. [...] The number of shares we repurchase, if any, during future periods will vary based on our cash position, potential uses of our cash, and market conditions.
evidence.subsection: LIQUIDITY AND CAPITAL RESOURCES

factors.summary: Capital expenditures are growing due to investments in software and data center construction, indicating confidence in future growth.

evidence.text: We used $17.8 million and $6.7 million for capital expenditures during the three months ended March 31, 2016 and March 31, 2015. This increase was primarily due to the purchase and development of software and the continued construction of a second data center.
evidence.subsection: LIQUIDITY AND CAPITAL RESOURCES

factors.summary: The company is maintaining its debt leverage with the revolving credit facility balance at $470.0 million, in compliance with financial covenants.

evidence.text: The outstanding balance on the revolving credit facility was $470.0 million as of March 31, 2016. We were in compliance with all of the credit facility's financial debt covenants as of March 31, 2016.
evidence.subsection: LIQUIDITY AND CAPITAL RESOURCES

factors.summary: Demand trends are mixed across different transportation modes.

evidence.text: Truckload volumes increased four percent, while intermodal opportunities were negatively impacted by the lower cost truck market.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended March 31, 2016 , Compared to Three Months Ended March 31, 2015

factors.summary: Order backlog or book-to-bill information is not explicitly provided.

evidence.text: No specific mention of backlog orders or book-to-bill metrics.
evidence.subsection: Introduction / ITEM 2. MANAGEMENT'S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND RESULTS OF OPERATIONS

factors.summary: Pricing power is weakening in some areas.

evidence.text: Lower rates charged to customers due to lower fuel costs.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended March 31, 2016 , Compared to Three Months Ended March 31, 2015

factors.summary: Revenue mix is shifting towards higher-margin services.

evidence.text: Net revenue margin increased due to lower transportation costs, particularly in truckload and LTL services.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended March 31, 2016 , Compared to Three Months Ended March 31, 2015

factors.summary: Volumes are growing in most transportation modes.

evidence.text: Truckload, LTL, ocean, and other logistics services volumes increased year-over-year.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended March 31, 2016 , Compared to Three Months Ended March 31, 2015

factors.summary: End markets are mixed with some growing faster than others.

evidence.text: Ocean transportation net revenues increased 16.9%, while air transportation net revenues decreased 10.8%.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended March 31, 2016 , Compared to Three Months Ended March 31, 2015

factors.summary: Input costs are declining, particularly fuel costs.

evidence.text: Decreased cost of capacity, primarily related to lower fuel costs.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended March 31, 2016 , Compared to Three Months Ended March 31, 2015

factors.summary: Labor costs are increasing due to headcount growth and payroll tax expenses.

evidence.text: Personnel expenses increased 8.8% due to a 5.5% increase in headcount and additional payroll tax expense.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended March 31, 2016 , Compared to Three Months Ended March 31, 2015

factors.summary: Margins are expanding, particularly in transportation services.

evidence.text: Net revenue margin increased to 19.7% from 16.8%, driven by lower transportation costs.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended March 31, 2016 , Compared to Three Months Ended March 31, 2015

factors.summary: Cost reduction initiatives are delivering measurable savings.

evidence.text: Operating expenses as a percentage of net revenues decreased slightly to 64.7% from 65.4%.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended March 31, 2016 , Compared to Three Months Ended March 31, 2015

factors.summary: Operational efficiency is improving.

evidence.text: Income from operations increased 9.4% and operating expenses as a percentage of net revenues decreased.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended March 31, 2016 , Compared to Three Months Ended March 31, 2015

factors.summary: Supply chain health is stable with available capacity and modest demand.

evidence.text: Truckload transportation costs decreased due to readily available capacity and modest demand.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended March 31, 2016 , Compared to Three Months Ended March 31, 2015

factors.summary: Capacity utilization is increasing in most transportation modes.

evidence.text: Higher volumes in most transportation modes offset by lower costs.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended March 31, 2016 , Compared to Three Months Ended March 31, 2015

factors.summary: Inventory levels are normalizing.

evidence.text: No specific mention of inventory levels, but sourcing revenues indicate stable product costs.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended March 31, 2016 , Compared to Three Months Ended March 31, 2015

factors.summary: No significant quality or safety issues reported.

evidence.text: No mention of product quality, safety incidents, or warranty costs.
evidence.subsection: Introduction / ITEM 2. MANAGEMENT'S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND RESULTS OF OPERATIONS

factors.summary: Forward guidance is cautious due to market conditions.

evidence.text: Management discusses risks like economic conditions and competition but does not provide specific guidance updates.
evidence.subsection: FORWARD-LOOKING INFORMATION

factors.summary: Management appears cautious due to market conditions.

evidence.text: Emphasis on risks such as fuel prices, competition, and regulations.
evidence.subsection: FORWARD-LOOKING INFORMATION

factors.summary: Near-term risks include fuel price volatility, competition, and regulatory changes.

evidence.text: Risks include fuel price increases or decreases, competition, and government regulations.
evidence.subsection: FORWARD-LOOKING INFORMATION

factors.summary: Long-term strategic direction is clear with investments in e-commerce capabilities.

evidence.text: Acquisition of Freightquote enhances e-commerce capabilities in LTL and truckload businesses.
evidence.subsection: OVERVIEW

factors.summary: Key sensitivities include fuel prices, volumes, and economic conditions.

evidence.text: Fuel prices, market demand, and economic conditions are critical factors impacting results.
evidence.subsection: FORWARD-LOOKING INFORMATION

factors.summary: Near-term catalysts include new customer contracts and margin expansion.

evidence.text: Increased net revenues from new customers in ocean transportation.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended March 31, 2016 , Compared to Three Months Ended March 31, 2015

factors.summary: Market share is stable with no significant gains or losses reported.

evidence.text: No specific mention of market share changes.
evidence.subsection: Introduction / ITEM 2. MANAGEMENT'S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND RESULTS OF OPERATIONS

factors.summary: Competitive dynamics are intense with new entrants and pricing pressures.

evidence.text: Competition and growth rates within the third party logistics industry are significant risks.
evidence.subsection: FORWARD-LOOKING INFORMATION

factors.summary: Differentiation is maintained through a strong global network and customer relationships.

evidence.text: Network offices provide a competitive advantage by building local relationships and penetrating markets effectively.
evidence.subsection: OVERVIEW

factors.summary: Customer concentration is low with a diverse customer base.

evidence.text: Top 100 customers represented 29% of total revenues, with no single customer over 2%.
evidence.subsection: OVERVIEW

factors.summary: Organic revenue growth is stable, as the company focuses on internal growth opportunities and possible acquisitions. The evidence indicates consistent organic growth with positive trends, such as higher volumes and margin improvements.

evidence.text: Organic revenue growth is stable as the company focuses on internal growth opportunities and possible acquisitions.
evidence.subsection: LIQUIDITY AND CAPITAL RESOURCES

evidence.text: Net revenues increased 7.3%, driven by higher volumes and margin improvements.
